description: Formalize Karpathy LLM Wiki operations into Alexandria, integrate Autoreason into Ralph, add ruff+vulture dead code gate

userStories.title: /ingest skill — source-to-wiki pipeline
userStories.description: As a knowledge worker, I want a /ingest skill that processes any source (URL, PDF, file) into structured Alexandria wiki pages so that knowledge compounds automatically instead of ad-hoc filing.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md created at ~/.claude/skills/ingest/SKILL.md with frontmatter matching existing skill format
Accepts a source path (local file or URL) as argument
Reads source content via appropriate tool (Read for local, WebFetch/Jina for URL)
Generates a summary page in wiki/ subdirectory of the relevant Alexandria domain (routes via ROUTING.md)
Updates the domain CONTEXT.md with a new entry linking to the summary
Cross-references: scans existing wiki pages for related entities and adds backlinks
Appends an entry to ~/alexandria/_system/logs/ingestion-log.md with timestamp, source, pages created/updated
Dry-run mode: /ingest --dry shows what would change without writing
Running /ingest on an already-ingested source updates existing pages rather than duplicating
userStories.notes: Model after Karpathy's ingest operation. Alexandria already has _system/ingestion/ for structured data (Plaid/Odoo). This skill handles unstructured sources (articles, PDFs, web pages). COMPLETED: v1.0.1 with 2 review rounds, 8 must-fix + 5 should-fix resolved.

userStories.title: /lint skill — Alexandria health checker
userStories.description: As a knowledge base maintainer, I want a /lint skill that finds stale, contradictory, and orphaned content in Alexandria so that the wiki stays trustworthy over time.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md created at ~/.claude/skills/alexandria-lint/SKILL.md
Scans all CONTEXT.md files across Alexandria domains for broken internal links (references to files that don't exist)
Detects orphan pages: files with zero inbound references from any CONTEXT.md or other wiki page
Detects stale pages: files not modified in 90+ days that reference time-sensitive data (prices, inventory, dates)
Detects contradictions: cross-checks GROUND_TRUTH.md entries against claims in wiki pages (flags mismatches)
Outputs a structured report with severity (critical/warning/info) and specific file:line references
Critical findings auto-appended to ~/alexandria/_system/ACTIVE_CORRECTIONS.md
Running /lint with no args scans all domains; /lint advanced scans only ~/alexandria/advanced/
userStories.notes: Maps to Karpathy's lint operation. Key differentiator: integrates with our existing GROUND_TRUTH.md and CORRECTIONS.md infrastructure. COMPLETED: v1.0.1 with 1 review round, 5 must-fix resolved.

userStories.title: /autoresearch skill — autonomous research-to-wiki loop
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want a /autoresearch skill that autonomously searches the web on a topic, identifies gaps, and files structured wiki pages into Alexandria so that research compounds without manual curation.
acceptanceCriteria: SKILL.md created at ~/.claude/skills/autoresearch/SKILL.md
Accepts a topic string as argument: /autoresearch 'IDG market pricing Q1 2026'
Round 1: Runs 3-5 parallel WebSearch queries, fetches top sources via Jina, extracts key findings
Round 2: Identifies gaps in coverage (missing perspectives, contradictions, unanswered questions), runs targeted follow-up searches
Round 3: Synthesizes findings into structured wiki pages with YAML frontmatter, source citations, and cross-references
Files output pages into the correct Alexandria domain (auto-routes via ROUTING.md based on topic)
Each generated page includes a sources section with URLs and access dates
Calls /ingest internally for each source processed (reuses US-001 pipeline)
Appends research session summary to ~/alexandria/_system/logs/research-log.md
Max 3 rounds to prevent runaway research loops; user can extend with /autoresearch --continue
userStories.notes: Extends Karpathy's AutoResearch + shl0ms Autoreason paper's methodology. Uses /ingest (US-001) as the filing backend. Three rounds mirrors the Autoreason convergence pattern. COMPLETED: v1.0.1 with 1 review round, 5 must-fix + 5 should-fix resolved.

userStories.title: Autoreason in Ralph REVIEW phase
userStories.description: As the Ralph autonomous agent, I want the REVIEW phase to use Autoreason's A/B/AB blind judging instead of single-reviewer adversarial review so that iterative refinement doesn't degrade output quality.
acceptanceCriteria: Ralph SKILL.md Phase 6 (REVIEW) updated to use Autoreason protocol
Three versions produced: A (unchanged implementation), B (adversarial revision by fresh Sonnet agent), AB (synthesis of A and B by third agent)
Blind Borda count: 2 fresh judge agents (no shared context with A/B/AB producers) rank all three versions without knowing which is which
If A (do-nothing) wins 2 consecutive rounds, loop stops — implementation is good enough
If B or AB wins, that version replaces A and the loop continues (max 3 rounds)
Judge prompt includes acceptance criteria and gate results but NOT which version is the incumbent
All versions and judge scores logged to tasks/ralph-autoreason-[story-id].md for auditability
Fallback: if Autoreason adds >5 minutes to REVIEW, degrade to current single-reviewer mode with a log entry
userStories.notes: Based on NousResearch/autoreason. Key insight: iterative self-refinement degrades because models hallucinate flaws to satisfy critique prompts. A/B/AB + blind judging eliminates this bias. Haiku 3.5 + autoreason scored perfect Borda in their benchmarks. COMPLETED: v2.0.0 Phase 6 rewrite with 1 review round, 4 must-fix + 4 should-fix resolved.

userStories.title: ruff + vulture pre-commit dead code gate
userStories.description: As a developer working on hermes-agent, I want a pre-commit hook that runs ruff and vulture on changed Python files so that dead code from vibe-coded sessions never lands in the repo.
acceptanceCriteria: Pre-commit hook installed at .git/hooks/pre-commit (or via pre-commit framework if already configured)
Hook runs ruff check on staged .py files only (not entire repo)
Hook runs vulture on staged .py files with --min-confidence 80 (reduces false positives)
Framework-aware whitelist for pytest, FastAPI, click patterns (vulture whitelist file at .vulture-whitelist.py)
Hook blocks commit if ruff finds errors (warnings allowed) or vulture finds dead code above confidence threshold
Clear error output showing file:line and what's dead
Hook runs in <5 seconds for typical commits (10-20 staged files)
requirements-dev.txt updated with ruff and vulture pinned versions
Hook can be bypassed with --no-verify for emergencies (standard git behavior, no custom flag)
userStories.notes: Inspired by @gabriberton tweet (680K views). Fits our Mandatory Feedback Gates pattern. ruff handles F401 (unused imports), F841 (unused vars). vulture catches unused functions/classes that ruff misses. COMPLETED: hook + whitelist + requirements-dev, 1 review round, 2 must-fix + 4 should-fix resolved.
